title: Wedded.
content:
A solemn thing it was, I said,
    A woman white to be,
    And wear, if God should count me fit,
    Her hallowed mystery.

    A timid thing to drop a life
    Into the purple well,
    Too plummetless that it come back
    Eternity until.
meaning:
Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Wedded."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
